subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Help the Word Workshop crew fix signs by snapping prefixes, suffixes, and base words into place.
lesson.objective: I can identify base words and use common prefixes and suffixes to read and understand new words.
lesson.concept_explanation: A base word carries the main meaning. A prefix goes before a base word and a suffix goes after it. Word parts can change how a word is read and what it means.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may grab too much of the word as the base word or attach a prefix or suffix to the wrong side.
lesson.real_world_connection: Word-part clues help readers understand classroom directions, story words, and science words.
lesson.lesson_summary: Find the base word, name the prefix or suffix, then blend the parts and check the meaning.
lesson.mini_lesson: Read the base word first. Add the prefix or suffix. Then explain how the new part changes the meaning.

worked_examples.text: Watch: In replay, re- means again and play is the base word.
Demo: In helpful, help is the base word and -ful means full of.
Demo: Build un + safe to make unsafe, which means not safe.

hint_ladder: Cover the prefix or suffix and read the base word first.
Ask what the prefix or suffix does to the meaning.
Check the word-part boundary before you build the whole word.

guided_practice.prompt: What is the base word in unhappy?
hints: Cover the prefix or suffix and read the base word first.
Ask what the prefix or suffix does to the meaning.
Check the word-part boundary before you build the whole word.
guided_practice.explanation: happy is correct because the word parts show the meaning of unhappy.
guided_practice.feedback: Yes — happy fits the word parts.

question_audio.text: What is the base word in unhappy?
question_audio.label: Read Question

misconception_bank.base_word_confusion: Learner treats the whole changed word as the base word.
misconception_bank.prefix_meaning_confusion: Learner does not connect re-, un-, or pre- to meaning.
misconception_bank.suffix_meaning_confusion: Learner does not connect -s, -ed, -ing, -ful, or -less to meaning.
misconception_bank.word_part_boundary_error: Learner splits the word at the wrong word-part boundary.

game_theme.mission_intro: Collect base words, prefixes, and suffixes to build new words in the Word Workshop.

story.text: Listen to this page. You are starting an English mission about Prefixes, Suffixes, and Base Words. Look at the story and get ready to read carefully. Think about the sounds, words, sentences, or details that matter. Press Start Mission when you are ready.

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Prefixes, Suffixes, and Base Words. Look for the reading or writing pattern on the page. Listen for word parts, important details, or sentence choices that help you decide. Remember to reread before you answer. Press Next to continue.

watch.text: This page lets you watch a worked example for Prefixes, Suffixes, and Base Words. Follow the reading or writing step shown on the page. Look back at the words and say the clue that helped. Press Next to continue.

demo.text: This demo helps you try Prefixes, Suffixes, and Base Words with support. Read the word, sentence, or passage first. Then use the model to explain your thinking. Press Next when you are ready for practice.

practice.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your English mission work on Prefixes, Suffixes, and Base Words. Think about the reading or writing strategy you used today. Keep listening, rereading, and checking your answer. Save your growth data to continue.
